root_text.iti75:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo sabbesaññeva na dātā hoti, samaṇabrāhmaṇakapaṇaddhikavanibbakayācakānaṁ annaṁ pānaṁ vatthaṁ yānaṁ mālāgandhavilepanaṁ seyyāvasathapadīpeyyaṁ.
root_text.iti75:3.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo avuṭṭhikasamo hoti.
root_text.iti75:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo ekaccānaṁ dātā hoti, ekaccānaṁ na dātā hoti samaṇabrāhmaṇakapaṇaddhikavanibbakayācakānaṁ annaṁ pānaṁ vatthaṁ yānaṁ mālāgandhavilepanaṁ seyyāvasathapadīpeyyaṁ.
root_text.iti75:4.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo padesavassī hoti.
root_text.iti75:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo sabbesaṁva deti, samaṇabrāhmaṇakapaṇaddhikavanibbakayācakānaṁ annaṁ pānaṁ vatthaṁ yānaṁ mālāgandhavilepanaṁ seyyāvasathapadīpeyyaṁ.
root_text.iti75:5.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo sabbatthābhivassī hoti.
root_text.iti75:5.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.
root_text.iti75:11.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti75:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti75:2.1: “Mendicants, these three individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.iti75:2.3: One like a rainless cloud, one who rains locally, one who rains all over.
translation_text.iti75:3.1: And how is an individual like a rainless cloud?
translation_text.iti75:3.2: It’s when an individual doesn’t give to anyone at all—whether ascetics and brahmins, paupers, vagrants, supplicants, or beggars—such things as food, drink, clothing, vehicles; garlands, fragrance, and makeup; and bed, house, and lighting.
translation_text.iti75:3.3: That’s how an individual is like a rainless cloud.
translation_text.iti75:4.1: And how does an individual rain locally?
translation_text.iti75:4.2: It’s when an individual gives to some but not to others—whether ascetics and brahmins, paupers, vagrants, supplicants, or beggars—such things as food, drink, clothing, vehicles; garlands, fragrance, and makeup; and bed, house, and lighting.
translation_text.iti75:4.3: That’s how an individual rains locally.
translation_text.iti75:5.1: And how does an individual rain all over?
translation_text.iti75:5.2: It’s when an individual gives to everyone—whether ascetics and brahmins, paupers, vagrants, supplicants, or beggars—such things as food, drink, clothing, vehicles; garlands, fragrance, and makeup; and bed, house, and lighting.
translation_text.iti75:5.3: That’s how an individual rains all over.
translation_text.iti75:5.4: These are the three individuals found in the world.”
translation_text.iti75:6.1: “They don’t share the food and drink
translation_text.iti75:6.6: they say, the meanest of men.
translation_text.iti75:10.5: rightly satisfies with food and drink
translation_text.iti75:11.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.
